operations.summary: Get Cisco cryptographic maps by revision
Get Cisco IPsec policy and peers
operations.notes:
Get policy and peers defined on a Cisco crypto map which is applied to specific interface (provided through the outputInterfaceName parameter) in the running config.
You can retrieve the interface names by calling the /devices/id/interfaces API.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of target device

operations.summary: Get Check Point VPN (IPSec) communities and gateways
operations.notes:
Get Check Point VPN (IPSec) Communities and gateways that partcipant in those communities.
Works on clusters and gateways, not on management servers.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of target device

protocol.description: The port number, for IP service: protocol is not specified.

min.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the minimum port number, ICMP Service: the ICMP type number.

max.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the maximum port number.

negate.description: Negates the defined port(s), that is: the defined port(s) are ignored, and only all other logs are analyzed.

timeout.description: The timeout value for the service in seconds

minSource.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the minimum source port number, ICMP Service: the ICMP type number.

maxSource.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the maximum source port number.
